title: NumPy Intro & Array Creation
objective: Install NumPy and understand why it's faster than Python lists.
concepts: Comparing list operations vs vectorized NumPy
ndarray properties: shape, ndim, dtype, size

exercise.description: Convert [1,2,3,4,5] to an array. Print shape, ndim, dtype. Compare speed: sum a list of 1 million numbers with Python vs NumPy (use time module).
exercise.solution:
```python
import numpy as np
import time

arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
print('Shape:', arr.shape, 'ndim:', arr.ndim, 'dtype:', arr.dtype)

# Speed comparison
lst = list(range(1_000_000))
np_arr = np.arange(1_000_000)

t0 = time.perf_counter()
sum(lst)
t1 = time.perf_counter()
print(f'List sum: {t1-t0:.4f}s')

t0 = time.perf_counter()
np_arr.sum()
t1 = time.perf_counter()
print(f'NumPy sum: {t1-t0:.4f}s')
```

homework: Explain broadcasting in your own words (even a rough first idea). We'll verify it later.

problems.q: Create a 2D array from a nested list [[1,2],[3,4]]. What is its shape?
Convert a list of floats to an array. What dtype does it get?

example1.title: Array creation and properties
example1.code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
print(arr.shape, arr.ndim, arr.dtype, arr.size)

application: NumPy is the foundation of pandas, scikit-learn, and scientific Python. Almost all numeric data in data science flows through NumPy arrays.
caseStudy: A financial model processes 1M price points. Using Python lists takes 30 seconds; vectorized NumPy completes in 0.3 seconds. The 100x speedup enables real-time dashboards.
visualization: Create a bar chart comparing Python list sum vs NumPy sum execution time for array sizes 10^4, 10^5, 10^6. Use matplotlib to visualize the speedup.
